info.title: Zadarma Teamsale CRM API
info.description:
Teamsale CRM API methods.

**Base URL:** `https://api.zadarma.com`

**Authorization:** Each request must include an `Authorization` header in the format `userKey:signature`. The signature is computed as:
1. Sort request params alphabetically by key
2. Build query string (RFC 1738)
3. Concatenate: `method_path + query_string + md5(query_string)`
4. `signature = base64(HMAC-SHA1(concatenated_string, secret_key))`

**Response format:** JSON by default; add `format=xml` to get XML.

**Rate limits:** 100 requests/min general; 3 requests/min for statistics methods. HTTP 429 is returned when the limit is exceeded.

ApiKeyAuth.description:
Authorization header format: `userKey:signature`.
Keys are generated in your personal account at https://my.zadarma.com/api/.
Signature algorithm: `base64(HMAC-SHA1(method + queryString + md5(queryString), secretKey))`
where params are sorted alphabetically and encoded with http_build_query (RFC 1738).

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable list of customers.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search across name, phones, description, address, zip, website, email, messengers, employees.

totalCount.description: Total number of customers matching search/filter.

created_by.description: ID of the user who created the record.

lead_created_at.description: Lead creation datetime (if created from a lead).

lead_created_by.description: User ID who created the lead.

post.description: Creates a new customer with the specified data.

labels.description: Labels array — each element must contain existing label ID (optional).

utms.description: Source tags array — each element must contain existing tag ID (optional).

custom_properties.description: Custom properties array — each element must contain id (or key) and value (optional).

key.description: Custom property unique key (alternative to id).

200.description: Successful response — returns the ID of the created customer.

get.description: Returns full customer data by ID, including phones, contacts, labels, UTMs, and custom properties.

created_by.description: ID of the user who created the record.

lead_created_at.description: Lead creation datetime (if created from a lead).

lead_created_by.description: User ID who created the lead.

put.description: Updates an existing customer by ID.

labels.description: Labels array — each element must contain existing label ID (optional).

utms.description: Source tags array — each element must contain existing tag ID (optional).

custom_properties.description: Custom properties array — each element must contain id (or key) and value (optional).

key.description: Custom property unique key (alternative to id).

200.description: Successful response — no body or empty success.

get.description: Returns all source tags and their usage statistics.

200.description: Successful response — array of source tags.

count.description: Number of clients and leads using this tag.

200.description: Successful response — returns the ID of the created source tag.

put.description: Updates an existing source tag by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a source tag from the system by ID.

get.description: Returns all labels and their usage statistics.

count.description: Number of clients/leads using this label.

200.description: Successful response — returns the created label.

count.description: Number of clients/leads using this label (0 on creation).

delete.description: Deletes a label from the system by ID.

get.description: Returns all additional custom properties defined for customers.
Returns all records in the client's timeline feed.

type.description: Record type: event, note, or call.

content.description: For notes: note text. For events: event identifier (e.g. CUSTOMER_CREATED, LEAD_CREATED).

user_id.description: ID of the user who created the record.

user_name.description: Name of the user who created the record.

call_phone.description: Phone number involved in the call.

call_record.description: Call recording URL (if recording is enabled).

attached_files.description: Files attached to a note (for note records).

post.description: Adds a text note to the client's timeline, with optional file attachments.

200.description: Successful response — returns the created feed item.

type.description: Record type (always note for created records).

user_id.description: ID of the user who created the record.

user_name.description: Name of the user who created the record.

put.description: Updates an existing text note in the client's timeline by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a note in the client's timeline by ID.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable and sortable list of leads.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search across lead name, phone, description, address, zip, website, email, messengers.

parameters.name: filter[responsible]
parameters.description: Filter by responsible user ID. Special values: null — assigned leads; -1 — raw (unassigned) leads; -2 — all leads.

parameters.name: filter[createdAfter]
parameters.description: Return only leads created after this datetime (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss).

parameters.name: filter[createdBefore]
parameters.description: Return only leads created before this datetime (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss).

totalCount.description: Total number of leads matching search/filter.

uncategorizedCount.description: Total number of raw (unassigned) leads matching search/filter.

lead_created_by.description: User ID who created the lead.

post.description: Creates a new lead. The `convert` parameter controls whether to create a lead, convert directly to a client, or cancel.

convert.description: 0 — create a lead; 1 — create a client directly; 2 — cancel (poor fit, no lead/client created).

labels.description: Labels — each element must contain existing label ID (optional).

utms.description: Source tags — each element must contain existing tag ID (optional).

custom_properties.description: Custom properties — each element must contain id (or key) and value (optional).

key.description: Custom property unique key (alternative to id).

200.description: Successful response — returns the ID of the created lead.

get.description: Returns full lead data by ID, including custom properties.

lead_created_by.description: User ID who created the lead.

put.description: Updates an existing lead by ID. The `convert` parameter can convert a lead to a client or mark as poor fit.

convert.description: 0 — do not convert; 1 — create a client; 2 — poor fit (delete lead).

labels.description: Labels — each element must contain existing label ID (optional).

utms.description: Source tags — each element must contain existing tag ID (optional).

custom_properties.description: Custom properties — each element must contain id (or key) and value (optional).

key.description: Custom property unique key (alternative to id).

get.description: Returns all CRM users with their profile, contact, and settings information.

is_superadmin.description: Whether the user is a super-administrator (1 or 0).

enabled.description: Whether the user is unblocked (1 or 0).

first_day.description: First day of the week: 0 — Sunday, 1 — Monday.

get.description: Returns a single CRM user by ID, including pending email change status.

is_superadmin.description: Whether the user is a super-administrator (1 or 0).

enabled.description: Whether the user is unblocked (1 or 0).

first_day.description: First day of the week: 0 — Sunday, 1 — Monday.

pending_email_change_request.description: True if an account email change has been sent but not yet confirmed.

get.description: Returns the work schedule for a CRM user, including schedule fixes and custom single working periods.

schedulePeriod.description: Schedule repeat frequency in days (e.g. 7 for weekly, 2 for alternating days).

scheduleWorkingHours.description: Base work schedule. Each entry is one working period.

index.description: Index in scheduleWorkingHours that was changed.

repeat_index.description: Schedule frequency index for future period changes.

deleted.description: 1 if the working period is fully deleted.

get.description: Returns all user groups with their types and permission sets.

permissions.description: Group permissions. Admins have full access — for admin groups this object is empty.

customers_view_all.description: View all clients including other users' allowed.

get.description: Returns a unified, paginated list of all contacts: clients, employees, leads, and users.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search across names and phone numbers of clients, leads, employees and users, including PBX extension.

200.description: Successful response — each contact has its own attribute set depending on contact_type.

totalCount.description: Total number of contacts matching search.

contacts.description: Array of contact objects. Each item's shape depends on contact_type: customer, employee, lead, or user.

get.description: Identifies a contact (client, employee, lead, or user) by phone number. Returns a single matching contact object.

200.description: Successful response — returns the matched contact. Shape depends on contact_type.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable and sortable list of deals.

totalCount.description: Total number of deals matching search/filter.

created_by.description: User ID who created the deal.

customer_id.description: ID of the client associated with the deal.

customer_is_lead.description: Flag indicating whether the associated client is a lead (1) or a customer (0).

customer_name.description: Name of the client associated with the deal.

customer_responsible_user.description: User ID responsible for the associated client.

post.description: Creates a new deal with the specified data.

customer_id.description: ID of the client to associate with the deal (optional, not used in PUT).

200.description: Successful response — returns the ID of the created deal.

get.description: Returns a single deal by ID.

created_by.description: User ID who created the deal.

customer_id.description: ID of the client associated with the deal.

customer_is_lead.description: Flag indicating whether the associated client is a lead (1) or a customer (0).

customer_name.description: Name of the client associated with the deal.

customer_responsible_user.description: User ID responsible for the associated client.

put.description: Updates an existing deal by ID. Note: customer_id cannot be changed via PUT.

get.description: Returns all records in the deal's feed/timeline.

type.description: Record type: event, note, or call.

content.description: For notes: note text. For events: event identifier (e.g. CUSTOMER_CREATED, LEAD_CREATED).

user_id.description: ID of the user who created the record.

user_name.description: Name of the user who created the record.

attached_files.description: Files attached to a note (for note records).

post.description: Adds a text note to the deal feed with optional file attachments.

200.description: Successful response — returns the created feed item.

type.description: Record type: event, note, or call.

content.description: For notes: note text. For events: event identifier (e.g. CUSTOMER_CREATED, LEAD_CREATED).

user_id.description: ID of the user who created the record.

user_name.description: Name of the user who created the record.

attached_files.description: Files attached to a note (for note records).

put.description: Updates the content of an existing text note by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a note in the deal feed by ID.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable and sortable list of tasks/events.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by task name, description, and comments to completed tasks.

parameters.name: filter[createdBy]
parameters.description: Filter by user ID who created the task.

parameters.name: filter[start]
parameters.description: Display tasks starting after this datetime (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss).

parameters.name: filter[end]
parameters.description: Display tasks ending before this datetime (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss).

parameters.name: filter[completed]
parameters.description: Set to 1 to also include completed tasks.

allDay.description: Whether this is an all-day task. Date is taken from start.

created_by.description: User ID who created the task.

phone.description: Phone number for the call (only when type = call).

call_done.description: Whether the call was made (only when type = call).

completed.description: Whether the task is marked as complete.

completed_comment.description: Comment left when completing the task.

members.description: Array of task member user IDs.

contact_type.description: Whether the attached entity is a customer or a lead.

customers.description: Clients and leads attached to the task.

post.description: Creates a new task with the specified data.

description.description: Task description in Quill Delta format (optional).

phone.description: Phone number for the call — only when type = call (optional).

200.description: Successful response — returns the ID of the created task.

get.description: Returns a single task by ID.

allDay.description: Whether this is an all-day task. Date is taken from start.

created_by.description: User ID who created the task.

phone.description: Phone number for the call (only when type = call).

call_done.description: Whether the call was made (only when type = call).

completed.description: Whether the task is marked as complete.

completed_comment.description: Comment left when completing the task.

members.description: Array of task member user IDs.

contact_type.description: Whether the attached entity is a customer or a lead.

customers.description: Clients and leads attached to the task.

put.description: Updates an existing task by ID.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable and sortable list of CRM call records.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by phone numbers and contact names (clients, employees, leads, users).

parameters.name: filter[dateFrom]
parameters.description: Show only calls made after this datetime (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss).

parameters.name: filter[dateTo]
parameters.description: Show only calls made before this datetime (YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss).

totalCount.description: Total number of calls matching search/filter.

user_id.description: User who initiated or accepted the call.

record_url_till.description: Datetime until which the recording link is active.

contact_type.description: Contact type: customer — client or lead (see is_lead); employee — client employee; user — CRM user.

contact_customer_id.description: Client or lead ID (if call is related to a client).

contact_employee_id.description: Employee ID (if call is related to an employee).

employee_customer_id.description: Parent client ID when contact_type = employee.

contact_user_id.description: User ID (if call is related to a CRM user).

is_lead.description: Whether the call is related to a lead.

record_urls.description: Array of recording links (present only when specifically requested).

get.description: Returns a file (or its download link) by file ID.

200.description: Successful response — file content or download link.
